tldr: During the quarter, this elite cohort of managers exhibited a clear rotation toward semiconductor infrastructure and wide-moat financial data providers, while actively taking profits in select mega-cap tech and consumer-facing names. The aggressive accumulation of semiconductor ETFs and individual chipmakers highlights a structural bet on AI hardware, contrasting with the notable trimming of software giant Microsoft and highly mixed flows in Amazon and Alphabet. Furthermore, defensive posturing was evident as managers exited or reduced exposure to healthcare, retail, and consumer discretionary equities, while piling into new, idiosyncratic ideas like Versant Media Group.

themes.heading: Leaning Into Semiconductors & AI Infrastructure
themes.detail: Managers aggressively initiated and added to semiconductor exposure, utilizing both broad ETFs and individual chipmakers to capture AI hardware tailwinds.

themes.heading: Accumulating Financial Moats & Idiosyncratic Media
themes.detail: There was strong consensus buying in payment networks and financial ratings agencies, signaling a preference for high-margin businesses, alongside a highly popular new consensus initiation in Versant Media Group.

themes.heading: Mega-Cap Tech Rebalancing
themes.detail: The cohort actively managed their massive mega-cap tech weightings, heavily trimming Microsoft while exhibiting highly mixed, two-way flows in Amazon and Alphabet.

themes.heading: Trimming Consumer & Healthcare Exposure
themes.detail: Investors leaned out of consumer discretionary, retail, and healthcare names, taking profits or cutting exposure in major managed care, airline, and restaurant stocks.
